hook: The AI gave you a 7-step framework when you just needed a straight answer. Again.
caption:
The AI gave you a 7-step framework when you just needed a straight answer. Again.

Here's what's actually happening:

AI defaults to "comprehensive" because it doesn't know if you're a beginner or an expert. It doesn't know if you want the whole map or just the next turn. So it gives you everything — just in case.

The result? You asked "should I send that email?" and got a 400-word breakdown with headers like "Considerations for Tone" and "Potential Outcomes."

You already KNEW the considerations. You just needed someone to say yes or no.

This is the generic AI tax. You pay it every time the tool doesn't know how your brain works.

Some people think in frameworks. Some people think in gut checks. Some need the full picture before they can move. Others need one clear action or they'll spiral.

None of that is wrong. But AI doesn't know which one you are — so it hedges by giving you all of them.

BootFile fixes this by building a profile of HOW you actually think. Not your job title. Not your industry. The way your brain processes decisions.

So instead of 7 steps, it knows you want the answer first, reasoning second, and absolutely no bullet points.

Small difference. Completely different experience.
